behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface with correct parameter names, types, and return type
behaviors.justification: The real failure is when the real LLM generates code with wrong signatures. A mock LLM passing validation tests would hide this failure. We must call the actual LLM and verify the generated code contains the correct signature.
behaviors.test_description: Call the LLM with a function specification, parse the returned code to extract the function signature, and verify that parameter names, types, and return type match the specification.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided test cases on first or subsequent attempt through refinement loop
behaviors.justification: The failure is that the real LLM generates code that doesn't pass tests and the system fails to execute or refine. This requires real LLM output (which may be incorrect) combined with real code execution (System-level subprocess). Mocking either component hides the actual failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the LLM to generate a function implementation, execute it against a test suite, and verify either immediate pass or successful convergence through refinement attempts.

behaviors.description: Refinement step includes test failure messages in the next LLM prompt so the model can learn from mistakes
behaviors.justification: This is pure prompt construction logic independent of LLM capability. We can mock the test runner to return specific failures and verify they appear in the refinement prompt. No real infrastructure is needed because the failure would be caught by testing the prompt-building function directly.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the test runner to return specific failure messages, invoke the refinement prompt construction function, and assert that the prompt text contains all failure details.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all three attempts and their test results
behaviors.justification: This is control flow logic: incrementing a counter and stopping at 3. The failure (wrong loop count or missing results in error) is caught by unit-testing the iteration logic with mocked LLM and test runner. Real infrastructure is not needed to verify the counter behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the LLM to consistently return bad code and test runner to return failures, trigger the loop, verify it stops after exactly 3 iterations, and assert the error response includes all 3 attempts with complete test results.

behaviors.description: Full generate-test-refine-test loop produces a working implementation for a function that single LLM call typically fails on
behaviors.justification: The failure is that the refinement loop doesn't actually improve the code or converge on a solution. This requires calling the real LLM for generation AND refinement (2 agentic steps), combined with real code execution to verify test results. Mocking the LLM would hide whether it actually learns from feedback. One full iteration (agentic) is the minimum; multiple iterations would be Workflow-level but not strictly necessary for the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the LLM to generate code for a function known to be difficult (first attempt will fail tests), execute tests to capture failures, refine using test failures in the prompt, execute tests again on refined code, and verify the result either passes or demonstrates measurable improvement.
